page: Google Cloud Platform ct5lp-hightpu-1t is a specialized accelerator instance belonging to the ct5lp server family. It is equipped with 24 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture and 48 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The primary hardware feature is the integration of a single Google TPU accelerator of the ct5lp model.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given the inclusion of a dedicated Google TPU, the ct5lp-hightpu-1t is designed for machine learning workloads, specifically model training and inference tasks optimized for TPU architectures. The 1:2 ratio of memory to vCPUs aligns with compute-heavy processing requirements. From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance provides a specialized hardware profile that consolidates dedicated compute resources and TPU acceleration, avoiding the overhead of local storage for architectures that rely on centralized data lakes or network storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct5lp-hightpu-1t is a specialized accelerator instance within the ct5lp family, designed for compute-intensive machine learning workloads. The server features 24 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 48 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. Its primary hardware differentiator is the inclusion of a single Google TPU accelerator of the ct5lp model. This configuration does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions for data persistence, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The resource profile is optimized for workloads that benefit from dedicated TPU acceleration, such as deep learning model training, tuning, and inference pipelines, offering a balanced allocation of dedicated host CPU resources and specialized matrix math hardware.
